articles.title: DMK MLA Anitha Radhakrishnan arrested over remarks against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
articles.sum: The arrest came shortly after the Madras High Court dismissed an anticipatory bail plea filed by Mr. Radhakrishnan in a case registered by the Authoor police

articles.title: Food and Civil Supplies Minister orders review of eligibility criteria for priority ration cards
articles.sum: The review will examine whether major changes are required in the existing norms related to the ownership of four-wheeler vehicles, the floor area of houses, and the landholding limits for applicants who seek priority ration cards.

articles.title: Defence Acquisition Council approves ₹52,000 crore worth military purchase
articles.sum: The DAC also approved Akash Tarang, MRSAM, Kamikaze drones and naval unmanned systems

articles.title: Legislative debates must focus on solutions, not criticism: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla
articles.sum: Lok Sabha Secretariat, and the Parliamentary Research and Training Institute for Democracies (PRIDE), in collaboration with the West Bengal Legislative Assembly is organising the two-day orientation programme for newly-elected members

articles.title: Government to summon Meta over Instagram ads promoting child sexual abuse material
articles.sum: The Centre had also asked Meta to explain why action shouldn't be initiated under the IT Act and rules over WhatsApp's new feature that may increase cybercrimes

articles.title: ORR facelift design to be discussed with residents and companies before works commence
articles.sum: The Minister inspected the spot, and his visit was part of discussions on the comprehensive redevelopment of ORR from Silk Board Junction to K.R. Puram Metro Station at an estimated cost of ₹450 crore

articles.title: 'Pakistan must stop backing terror': India says Indus Waters Treaty 'in abeyance'; conveys views on Teesta to Bangladesh
articles.sum: India's stance on the Indus Waters Treaty remains firm, with the agreement suspended due to Pakistan's continued support for cross-border terrorism. The MEA reiterated this position, emphasizing Pakistan's need to cease its sponsorship of terror. Meanwhile, India has communicated...

articles.title: Maharashtra's Uniform Civil Code: Committee to draft legislation in two weeks
articles.sum: Maharashtra is set to establish a committee within two weeks to draft legislation for a Uniform Civil Code. This move follows an announcement that the state will implement UCC, with a retired high court judge to head the drafting panel. The UCC aims to create a common legal frame...

articles.title: 'Gravest threat from Modi-Shah regime': Opposition writes to CJI, raises concerns over SIR
articles.sum: Twenty-four opposition parties and an independent MP have penned a letter to the Chief Justice of India, voicing grave concerns over the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process and the Election Commission's perceived partisan role. The joint communication highlights a lack of tr...

articles.title: 'E20 petrol forcibly enforced': Kejriwal seeks public inputs for letter to PM Modi, says country now 'experimental lab'
articles.sum: Aam Aadmi Party chief Arvind Kejriwal has accused the Centre of "forcibly imposing" 20% ethanol-blended petrol (E20), claiming it damages vehicles and reduces mileage. He plans to write to Prime Minister Modi about widespread public anger. This follows similar criticism from Karn...

articles.title: Japan backs India’s 2027 target for Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project: All you need to know
articles.sum: Japan has reaffirmed its strong backing for India's high-speed rail ambitions, supporting the 2027 target for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor's initial operations. This commitment, alongside a new mobility partnership, signals deeper cooperation in advanced transport. Prime Ministe...

articles.title: What news reports from 1600s tell us about life in Mughal India
articles.sum: Thousands of forgotten news reports are reshaping our understanding of Aurangzeb and the Mughal world.
